description: Electron desktop dashboard MVP — login, Kanban board, run detail with live terminal, approve/reject flow using mock data

userStories.title: Shared TypeScript types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the shared TypeScript interfaces so all components have a consistent contract.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/types.ts exports RunStatus union type: 'queued' | 'running' | 'awaiting_approval' | 'pr_opened' | 'done' | 'failed'
src/lib/types.ts exports Run interface with fields: id, issue_number, issue_title, repo, status, provider, model, started_at, turns, test_result, pr_url
src/lib/types.ts exports SSEEvent interface with fields: type ('log' | 'turn' | 'test' | 'status'), timestamp, prefix, content
src/lib/types.ts exports User interface with fields: login, avatar_url, name
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mock data for offline development
userStories.description: As a developer, I need mock data so the frontend can render without a backend.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/mocks.ts exports MOCK_RUNS: array of 3+ Run objects covering running, awaiting_approval, and done statuses
src/lib/mocks.ts exports MOCK_SSE_EVENTS: array of 5+ SSEEvent objects covering log, turn, test, and status types
Mock data imports types from src/lib/types.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Electron preload and contextBridge
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the preload script to expose IPC channels to the renderer via contextBridge.
acceptanceCriteria: electron/preload.ts uses contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld to expose electronAPI with invoke and on methods
TypeScript declaration for window.electronAPI is available to the renderer
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: IPC typed wrapper functions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed wrapper functions for all IPC channels so React components have a clean API.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/ipc.ts exports typed functions: getRuns, getRun, approveRun, rejectRun, getConfig, saveConfig, getMe
Each function calls window.electronAPI.invoke with the correct channel name (runs:list, runs:get, runs:approve, runs:reject, config:get, config:save, auth:me)
Return types match the interfaces from types.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Electron main process with mock IPC handlers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Electron main process to create the window and register IPC handlers returning mock data.
acceptanceCriteria: electron/main.ts creates a BrowserWindow loading the Vite dev server (dev) or built files (prod)
Registers IPC handlers for: runs:list, runs:get, runs:approve, runs:reject, config:get, config:save, auth:me, auth:login, auth:logout, daemon:status
All IPC handlers return mock data for MVP
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Daemon management stub
userStories.description: As a developer, I need daemon spawn/kill stubs so the lifecycle is wired but does nothing in MVP.
acceptanceCriteria: electron/daemon.ts exports spawnDaemon and killDaemon functions
spawnDaemon logs a message and returns without spawning a process
killDaemon is a no-op
Functions are called on app ready and app quit in main.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: OAuth auth module stub
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the auth module to register the deep link protocol and provide mock auth for MVP.
acceptanceCriteria: electron/auth.ts registers auto-issue:// deep link protocol
auth:login IPC handler opens system browser to GitHub OAuth URL (or logs in MVP)
auth:me IPC handler returns a hardcoded mock user object for MVP
auth:logout IPC handler is a no-op for MVP
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SSE hook for real-time logs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a React hook that connects to SSE and accumulates log events.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/sse.ts exports useSSE(runId: string | null) returning { events: SSEEvent[], connected: boolean }
Opens EventSource to http://localhost:8080/api/runs/${runId}/logs when runId is not null
Accumulates events in state (append, never replace)
Sets connected: true after first onopen
Closes connection on unmount or when runId changes
Also exports useMockSSE that replays MOCK_SSE_EVENTS with timed delays
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Login page
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean login screen to authenticate with GitHub before accessing the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: src/pages/Login.tsx renders centered vertically and horizontally on --bg background
Shows logo with pulsing dot animation and 'auto-issue' in Azeret Mono
Shows muted subtitle 'Sign in to access your dashboard'
'Sign in with GitHub' button with GitHub SVG icon, --accent background, dark text
Button calls window.electronAPI.invoke('auth:login')
After auth:success event, navigates to Dashboard using React Router
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: ProviderBadge component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see which AI provider is running a task.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/ProviderBadge.tsx accepts provider ('openai' | 'anthropic' | 'gemini') and optional model string
Renders bracket pattern: [ openai ] or [ openai · gpt-4o ] if model is provided
Uses --accent-flat background, rgba(0,230,118,0.15) border, --accent text
Font: Azeret Mono 11px, letter-spacing 0.08em, padding 2px 8px, border-radius 4px
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: RunCard component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a summary card for each run showing issue, status, provider, and timing.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/RunCard.tsx accepts run: Run and onClick: () => void
Shows issue number #N, ProviderBadge, and status label [ STATUS ] with correct color per status
Left border 1px colored by status (queued→muted, running→amber, awaiting_approval→accent, pr_opened→blue, done→accent, failed→red)
Shows issue title truncated to one line
Footer shows turns count, elapsed time, retry count
If status is running, shows a live timer from started_at using setInterval
If test_result exists, shows test badge: checkmark tests (green), x tests (red), or dash tests (muted)
Running status shows CSS scanner line animation
Hover changes background from --bg to --bg2
All labels use Azeret Mono 10px, letter-spacing 0.12em, all-caps
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: UserMenu component
userStories.description: As a logged-in user, I want to see my avatar and access a sign-out dropdown.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/UserMenu.tsx accepts user: User
Shows circular avatar 28px from user.avatar_url with --border ring
Click opens dropdown anchored to the right with username and Sign out option
Sign out calls window.electronAPI.invoke('auth:logout') and navigates to Login
Dropdown closes when clicking outside (document click listener)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: KanbanBoard component
userStories.description: As a user, I want runs organized in status columns to see agent state at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx accepts runs: Run[] and onRunClick: (id: string) => void
Renders 5 columns: Queued, Running, Awaiting Approval, Done (includes pr_opened and done), Failed
Each column header shows name + run count (N)
Empty columns show 'No runs' in muted text
Horizontal scroll if columns overflow
Renders RunCard for each run in the appropriate column
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: AgentTerminal component
userStories.description: As a user, I want a terminal-style live log of agent activity with auto-scroll and status bar.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/AgentTerminal.tsx accepts runId: string
Uses useSSE(runId) or useMockSSE for demo to get events
Renders titlebar with fake traffic lights and label [ AGENT LOG · ISSUE #N ] in Azeret Mono all-caps muted
Each event renders as a line with timestamp (HH:MM:SS), 4-char prefix (INFO/WARN/ERR/T·NN/OK/FAIL/DONE), and content with colors per design system
New lines animate in with opacity 0→1 + translateY 4px→0 in 200ms (CSS keyframes only)
Auto-scrolls to latest line; Pause scroll button appears when user scrolls up
Blinking cursor while connected is true and status is running
Status bar shows: status pill + current turn + model + live elapsed time
Font: Azeret Mono 13px, background --bg2, border --border-mid
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: ApprovePanel component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to approve or reject an agent's work when it requires human review.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/ApprovePanel.tsx accepts run: Run, onApproved: () => void, onRejected: () => void
Only renders when run.status is awaiting_approval
Shows title 'Waiting for your approval' and subtitle about reviewing the PR
Shows clickable PR link 'View PR #N on GitHub' in blue from run.pr_url
Approve button: green-tinted flat background, calls approveRun(run.id) then onApproved()
Reject button: ghost with red border, calls rejectRun(run.id) then onRejected()
Both buttons show loading spinner and disable during API call
Background: rgba(167,139,250,0.06), border: rgba(167,139,250,0.2), border-radius 8px
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Dashboard page
userStories.description: As a user, I want the main dashboard to show all runs in a Kanban board with polling updates.
acceptanceCriteria: src/pages/Dashboard.tsx calls getMe() on mount and renders UserMenu in the nav
Nav shows [ AUTO-ISSUE ] branding in Azeret Mono
Polls getRuns() every 5 seconds via setInterval (mock data for MVP)
Renders KanbanBoard with the runs
Shows agent counter [ N / 12 AGENTS RUNNING ] above the board
Clicking a card navigates to /runs/${run.id} via React Router
Loading state: 3 skeleton cards with --bg3 background, opacity 0.5, no shimmer
Empty state: 'No active runs. Label a GitHub issue with' + inline [ auto-issue ] badge
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: RunDetail page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click into a run and see its full details, live terminal, and approval panel.
acceptanceCriteria: src/pages/RunDetail.tsx receives id from React Router params
Shows back link to dashboard at top
Header shows issue number, title, status label, and ProviderBadge
Shows repo link, turn count, and relative start time
Fetches run via getRun(id) on mount
If run.status is awaiting_approval, renders ApprovePanel above the terminal
Renders AgentTerminal with run.id, occupying remaining viewport height
After approve/reject, re-fetches run and updates local state
Typecheck passes
